rulings.Case Title: SOO MING LAI VS. MARIA NGUYEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons By Cross Defendant Helena Mew And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 19, 2019 line CROSS DEFENDANT HELENA MEW Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons By Cross Defendant Helena Mew Hearing Required. Helena Mew and David Freeman to be present, unless the parties agree that the motion is moot in light of filing of the Second Amended Complaint and issuance of new summons. No appearance is required if parties notify the Court no later than 8:00 a.m. on September 19, 2019 that the motion may be taken off calendar for the reasons stated above by sending an e-mail to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. No other communications to this address are authorized. =(501/RBU)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 19, 2019 line 5 DEFENDANT JAMES BANKS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is transferred to Department 505 to be heard by the Honorable Harold Kahn at 9:00 a.m. =(501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH HOLMES VS. ELIZABETH KAINOA LOSA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 19, 2019 line DEFENDANT RAYMOND FLORES, RICHARD FLORES MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Summary Adjudication is DENIED. There are triable issues of fact regarding defendants' status as licensees vs. tenants. The Court will not disregard a party's inconsistent discovery responses and declarations absent a reasonable explanation for the discrepancy. See D'amico v. Board of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal.3d 1, Ahn v. Kumho Tire USA (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 133, Scalf v. D.B. Log Homes, Inc. (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 1510. The moving party failed to provide any explanation for the discrepancy between the original discovery responses (including a deposition) and their later responses and declarations, let alone a reasonable explanation. See Declarations of Raymond Flores and Richard Flores. =(501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. ROSITA GUZMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendant'S Memorandum Of Costs Or In The Alternative Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 19, 2019 line PLAINTIFF THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendant'S Memorandum Of Costs Or In The Alternative Tax Costs Both parties move to tax in full the costs sought by the other's memorandum. Which party is "prevailing" depends on how CCP §1032(a)(4) is read. With a clean slate, I might well have determined that plaintiff was the prevailing party, because it recovered "other than monetary relief" (possession and back rent). (Id.) However, plaintiff dismissed its complaint without a costs waiver, and appellate law holds that, given such a dismissal, "the trial court lacks discretion to deny prevailing party status" to defendant. (Charton v. Harkey (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 730, 747.) In any event, plaintiff's motion to tax is granted in full because defendant's costs submission is erroneous, incoherent and unsupported by evidence. =(501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. ROSITA GUZMAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 19, 2019 line DEFENDANT ROSITA GUZMAN MOTION TO TAX COSTS Both parties move to tax in full the costs sought by the other's memorandum. Which party is "prevailing" depends on how CCP §1032(a)(4) is read. With a clean slate, I might well have determined that plaintiff was the prevailing party, because it recovered "other than monetary relief" (possession and back rent). (Id.) However, plaintiff dismissed its complaint without a costs waiver, and appellate law holds that, given such a dismissal, "the trial court lacks discretion to deny prevailing party status" to defendant. (Charton v. Harkey (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 730, 747.) In any event, plaintiff's motion to tax is granted in full because defendant's costs submission is erroneous, incoherent and unsupported by evidence. =(501/RBU)
